title: oxystophyllum oligadenium
parts: Oxystophyllum oligadenium has many medicinal parts, such as leaves, roots, and rhizomes. The leaves of Oxystophyllum oligadenium are rich in flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The roots and rhizomes of this plant contain a variety of medicinal constituents, including glycosides and phenolic compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever and respiratory issues. Additionally, the plant's flowers are also used in traditional medicine, containing compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are valued for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
side_effects: Oxystophyllum oligadenium can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and respiratory problems. Ingesting the plant's leaves or stems can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, while prolonged exposure to its essential oils may lead to headaches and dizziness. Improper use of Oxystophyllum oligadenium may also cause interactions with certain medications, exacerbating conditions like high blood pressure or diabetes. In severe cases, ingesting large quantities of the plant can cause cardiac issues, such as arrhythmias or even cardiac arrest.
